steps.title: Simplify the given line
steps.content: Start with $3(x-n)=9y+15n$. Expanding gives $3x-3n=9y+15n$, so $3x-9y=18n$. Divide by $3$ to get $$x-3y=6n.$$

steps.title: Use the no-solution condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution when the lines are parallel and distinct. The second equation must have the same $x-3y$ left side but a different constant from $6n$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content: Choice A is the same line, so it would give infinitely many solutions. Choices B and C use $x+3y$, so they are not parallel to $x-3y=6n$. Choice D, $x-3y=3n$, is parallel to the original and distinct because $n>0$, so $3n\ne6n$. Choice D is correct.
